releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.5

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed unreadable validation errors in the Create New Project dialog when required fields are missing.
releases.name: v1.3.5

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.4

## Bug Fixes

- Use window-qualified timer fallbacks so Obsidian popout windows remain compatible.
releases.name: v1.3.4

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.3

## Fixes

- Address the Obsidian community source review failure by removing the unsafe save-button `innerHTML` write.
- Replace browser `confirm()` with an Obsidian modal for discarding inbox items.
- Stop detaching plugin leaves during unload so user-moved leaves keep their workspace placement.
- Await or explicitly handle scanner-flagged async workspace and event work.
- Raise `minAppVersion` to `1.7.2`, matching the Obsidian APIs already used by Flow.
- Replace deprecated `MarkdownRenderer.renderMarkdown` calls with `MarkdownRenderer.render`.
- Remove the deprecated `builtin-modules` dev dependency.

## Maintenance

- Add a community review regression test covering the scanner patterns that failed this review.
releases.name: v1.3.3

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.2

## What's New

- Refresh the community listing metadata and README copy for the stable release.
- Add a GitHub Actions release workflow that builds, verifies, attests, and uploads release assets.

## Improvements

- Route cover image requests through Obsidian's request API.
- Improve popout window handling and background async task reporting.
- Move more static view and modal styling into the stylesheet.
- Tighten scanner typings and remove stale bundled package references.
releases.name: v1.3.2

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.1

Security dependency cleanup for the stable 1.3 release.

## Security

- Removed the bundled `obsidian-dataview` dependency tree from Flow's runtime bundle. Flow now discovers Dataview through Obsidian's installed plugin API when available.
- Removed unused `@anthropic-ai/sdk` dependency after the OpenRouter-only cover image path.
- Updated vulnerable transitive development dependencies reported by `npm audit`: `ajv`, `brace-expansion`, and `diff`.
- Confirmed `npm audit` reports zero vulnerabilities.

## Verification

- npm run format
- npm run build
- npm test
- npm audit --json
releases.name: v1.3.1

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.3.0

This release promotes the recent beta work to stable.

## What's New

- Redesigned inbox processing interface with faster keyboard-driven workflows.
- Added context tag extraction, filtering, and configurable context tag prefix support.
- Improved focus, sphere, waiting-for, and someday views for the updated GTD workflow.
- Added inbox tag autocomplete, project/person creation refinements, and sub-project creation support.
- Updated dependencies and security overrides from the beta/main branches.
releases.name: v1.3.0

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.2.2

## What's New

 - Create Person command — Create person notes directly from the command palette, with Templater support for person templates
 - Auto-select single sphere — When only one sphere is configured, it's now automatically selected during inbox processing and project creation. Thanks to @DanValnicek for the report. (#38)
releases.name: v1.2.2

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.2.1

## Bug Fixes

- Replace brittle this.containerEl.children[1] with the proper ItemView.contentEl API across all views. Huge thanks to @DanValnicek for the report and detailed investigation.
releases.name: v1.2.1

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.2.0

## What's New

- Templater support for project templates — If you use the https://github.com/SilentVoid13/Templater plugin, Flow now
automatically processes Templater syntax (<% ... %>) in your project template after creating the file. You can mix Flow's own {{ }} variables with any Templater commands. Templater is entirely optional.
releases.name: v1.2.0

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Release 1.1.3

## Bug Fix

- Fixed an issue where actions associated with multiple spheres couldn't be added to Focus easily. Fixes issue #31 (thanks for the report @shimanotaka).
releases.name: v1.1.3

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Flow 1.1.2

## What's New

- File and folder settings now show suggestions as you type
- Kinda a bugfix too: You can now create projects without specifying a next action upfront

## Improvements

- Obsidian-compatible dates: Frontmatter dates now use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:00) which Obsidian recognises natively. Thanks @cr7pt0gr4ph7!

## Security patches (non-critical)

- Fixed urllib3 vulnerability (CVE-2026-21441)
- Updated preact dependency

releases.author: tavva
releases.description: Beta release v1.2.0-beta.1
releases.name: Beta v1.2.0-beta.1

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
### Flow 1.1.1 Release Notes

### Bug Fix

- **Fixed sphere commands**: Sphere commands in the command palette now update immediately when sphere settings change, without requiring a restart.
releases.name: v1.1.1

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
### Flow 1.1.0 Release Notes

### New Features

- **Template placeholders**: Custom project templates now support `{{date}}`, `{{time}}` and `{{status}}` placeholders that get replaced when creating new projects.

- **Project descriptions**: When processing inbox items, Flow now preserves the AI-generated project description and includes it in the created project note.

Thanks to [@cr7pt0gr4ph7](https://github.com/cr7pt0gr4ph7) for contributing both features!

### Bug Fixes

- **Fixed sphere view bug**: Projects no longer disappear from sphere view when completing a focus action.

### Settings Changes

- **Renamed setting**: "Default Inbox File" now named correctly.
- **Changed default inbox**: The default inbox filename is now `Inbox.md` instead of the previous value.

### Documentation

- Added installation badge and button to README
- Added instructions for installing beta releases
- Clarified that LLM usage is only for cover image generation
- Added link to hot reload plugin in contribution docs
releases.name: v1.1.0

## What's Changed

### Improvements

- **Auto-create inbox folders**: When capturing to inbox, Flow now automatically creates any missing folders in the inbox path. Previously, the capture would fail if the configured inbox folder didn't exist.

### Housekeeping

- Removed the "Review projects" command which was not being used.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Flow 1.0.0

Flow is now open source! 🎉

This release marks a complete rewrite of Flow, with a new architecture resolving some previous issues, removing dependencies on other plugins, and adding many new features. Flow now runs entirely on its own.

## What's New

### Revamped Focus View (replacing the "Hotlist")
Your curated list of what to work on right now.
- Pin important actions to the top
- Drag to reorder priorities
- See completed items from today
- Project names displayed above actions
- Automatic daily clearing (configurable)

### Better Sphere Views, supporting subprojects
Organise your life into areas (work, personal, etc.) with dedicated views.
- Hierarchical project display with subprojects
- Priority sections (P1, P2, P3)
- Planning mode — click actions to add them to your focus
- Filter and search across projects
- Edit project priority inline

### Waiting For View
Track everything you're waiting on others to complete.
- Aggregated `[w]` items from across your vault
- Filter by sphere
- Quick completion when items come back

### Someday/Maybe View
A dedicated space for future possibilities.
- Review paused projects and aspirational items
- Move items to next actions when ready

### Cover Images generated by AI (optional!)
Generate unique cover images for your projects.
- Automatic generation during project creation
- Manual generation via command
- Images display in sphere view and project files

### Improved Inbox Processing
- Keyboard shortcuts throughout (Ctrl+1-4 for spheres, Ctrl+S to save, more to come here)
- Accordion behaviour for cleaner interface
- Create new projects directly from processing flow
- Loading indicators for AI operations

### Better Data Storage
- Focus data now stored in `flow-focus-data/focus.md` (syncs across devices)
- Automatic migration from legacy `#flow-planned` tags
- JSONL format for reliability

## Breaking Changes

- Legacy `#flow-planned` tags are automatically migrated

## Privacy

AI features remain optional. Flow works fully without any API keys configured. When AI is used, data is sent only to your configured provider. No telemetry.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://github.com/tavva/flow)
- [Report Issues](https://github.com/tavva/flow/issues)
- [Discussions](https://github.com/tavva/flow/discussions)

- Fixed: Don't allow settings to be updated when we're not ready (namely when our dependant plugins aren't installed)
releases.name: Release 0.6.5

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Changes in 0.6.4
- Fixed: Release process in closed repo now triggers release in public repo
- Improved local testing environment
- Slight docs improvements
releases.name: Release 0.6.4

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
# Changes in 0.6.3

- Fixed: creating projects failed when frontmatter.tags contained non-strings
- Misc: moved to Bun for development and build
- Misc: added some developer tooling for testing against new vaults
- Misc: corrected release process
- Misc: applied various security patches for dependencies
  - Bump svelte from 4.2.18 to 4.2.19
  - Bump micromatch from 4.0.7 to 4.0.8
  - Bump cross-spawn from 7.0.3 to 7.0.6

releases.author: tavva
releases.description:
- Added 'export planned tasks' to enable integration with other apps
- Fix review issues in preparation for public release

releases.author: tavva
releases.description: First public release!
